services.name: pub.file:bytesToFile
services.description: WmPublic. Writes the specified byte array to a file.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the file to which to write the byte array.

input_parameters.name: bytes
input_parameters.description: Byte[] The byte array to write to the file specified in the filename parameter.

input_parameters.name: append
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies whether to append or overwrite if the specified file already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if file already exists. Set to: true to append to the file if the file already exists. false to overwrite the file if the file already exists.

output_parameters.name: length
output_parameters.description: String Number of bytes written to the file.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:bytesToFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canWritePaths parameter. If the specified path is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:checkFileExistence
services.description: WmPublic. Checks if a specified file exists, and if the file exists, checks whether the file name represents a file or a directory.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the file to be checked.

output_parameters.name: exists
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether the specified fileName exists or not. A value of: true indicates that the specified fileName exists. false indicates that the specified fileName does not exist.

output_parameters.name: isDirectory
output_parameters.description: String Indicates whether the specified fileName is a file or a directory. true indicates that the specified fileName is a directory. false indicates that the specified fileName is a file.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:checkFileExistence service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. If the specified path is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:copyFile
services.description: WmPublic. Copies a file from one directory to another.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the file to be copied.

input_parameters.name: targetDirectory
input_parameters.description: String Directory to which the file specified in the fileName parameter will be copied.

input_parameters.name: appendTimestamp
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies whether the current timestamp will be appended to the target filename. Set to: true to append a timestamp to the target fileName in the yyyyMMddHHmmss format. false to omit a timestamp from the target fileName.

output_parameters.name: targetFileName
output_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the target file to which the fileName parameter is copied.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:copyFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter and the input targetDirectory against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canWritePaths parameter. If the specified file name or directory is not in the respective allowed lists, or if an invalid file path is specified, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameters, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:deleteFile

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the file to be deleted.

output_parameters.name: status
output_parameters.description: String Specifies the status of the delete operation. A value of: true indicates that the deletion succeeded. false indicates that the deletion failed.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:deleteFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canDeletePaths parameter. If the specified path is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:getFile
services.description: WmPublic. Retrieves a specified file from the local file system.

input_parameters.name: filename
input_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the file in the local file system (for example c:\rubicon\document.xml).

input_parameters.name: loadAs
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Form in which you want the getFile service to make the contents of the file available to subsequent services. Set to: bytes to return the file as a byte array. Use this option if the contents of the file will be used as input to a service that operates on whole documents (for example, pub.xml:queryXMLNode). This is the default. stream to return the file as an input stream. Use this option if the contents of the file will be used as input to a service that can process a document incrementally (for example, pub.xml:getXMLNodeIterator). string to return the file as a string. reader to return the file as a reader.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Character set in which the file is encoded. Specify an IANA-registered character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). This information is required to correctly convert the String object to bytes when performing a get. If no value is specified or if the value is set to autoDetect, the service uses the default operating system encoding. If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception.

input_parameters.name: bufferSize
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Buffer size (in bytes) to use if you are loading an InputStream (that is, loadAs=stream). The default is 4096 bytes.

input_parameters.name: largeFile
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Indicates whether the file specified in the input is a large file or not. As large files are loaded as a stream, set this parameter to true and the loadAs parameter to stream to get larger files. The default value is false. Note: Files larger than the system memory are considered as large files.

output_parameters.name: body
output_parameters.description: Document Document (IData object) containing the file as a byte[ ], InputStream, string, or reader. The body parameter will contain one of the following keys, depending on how the loadAs parameter was set:

output_parameters.name: bytes
output_parameters.description: byte[ ]Conditional. Returns file contents in a byte array if the loadAs parameter is set to bytes.

output_parameters.name: stream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream Conditional. Returns file contents as an InputStream if the loadAs parameter is set to stream.

output_parameters.name: reader
output_parameters.description: java.io.Reader Conditional. Returns file contents as a reader if the loadAs parameter is set to reader.

output_parameters.name: string
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Returns file contents as a string if the loadAs parameter is set to string.

usage_notes: Usage Notes The getFile service does not automatically generate an XML node from the contents of the file. To generate an XML node, the output from this service must be passed to the pub.xml:xmlStringToXMLNode service. The pub.file:getFile service first checks the value of the watt.security.pub.getFile.checkReadAllowed parameter to determine whether it must validate the requested file path against the paths listed in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. If watt.security.pub.getFile.checkReadAllowed is set to true, the pub.file:getFile service checks whether the specified filename is in the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. If the filename appears in the allowed list, the service retrieves the file. If the filename is not in the list, the service throws an exception. If watt.security.pub.getFile.checkReadAllowed is set to false, the pub.file:getFile service retrieves the specified file without checking the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. For more information about the watt.server.file.canReadPaths server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:getFileProperties
services.description: WmPublic. Retrieves properties for a specified file, such as creation date, last modified date, file size, and file type.

input_parameters.name: filePath
input_parameters.description: String. The absolute path of the file for which you want to retrieve properties. For example, c:\rubicon\document.xml.

output_parameters.name: createdDate
output_parameters.description: String. The file creation timestamp in the yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss format.

output_parameters.name: modifiedDate
output_parameters.description: String. The last modified timestamp in the yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss format.

output_parameters.name: fileSize
output_parameters.description: String. The size of the file in bytes.

output_parameters.name: fileType
output_parameters.description: String. The type or extension of a file.

usage_notes: Usage Notes The pub.file:getFileProperties service first checks the value of the watt.security.pub.getFile.checkReadAllowed parameter to determine whether it must validate the requested file path against the paths listed in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. If watt.security.pub.getFile.checkReadAllowed is set to true, the pub.file:getFileProperties service checks whether the specified filePath is in the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. If the filePath appears in the allowed list, the service retrieves the file properties. If the filePath is not in the list, the service throws an exception. If watt.security.pub.getFile.checkReadAllowed is set to false, the pub.file:getFileProperties service retrieves properties of the specified file without checking the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. For more information about the watt.server.file.canReadPaths server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:listFiles
services.description: WmPublic. List all the files in a specified directory.

input_parameters.name: directory
input_parameters.description: String[ ] Name of the directory that is to be queried.

input_parameters.name: filter
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Filter string for the file list (for example, *.java or EDI*.tx*).

output_parameters.name: fileList
output_parameters.description: String Names of the files in the directory specified in the directory parameter. Only the file names are returned; not the full path name. filel]List does not list subdirectories or recursively list subdirectory content.

output_parameters.name: numFiles
output_parameters.description: String Number of files in the specified directory.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:listFiles service validates the value of the directory against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter. If the specified directory is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters

services.name: pub.file:moveFile
services.description: WmPublic. Moves a file from one directory to another.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String The absolute path name of the file to be moved.

input_parameters.name: targetDirectory
input_parameters.description: String Name of the directory to which the file specified in the fileName parameter is to be moved.

input_parameters.name: appendTimeStamp
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies whether the current timestamp is to be appended to the file name after the file is moved to the target directory. Set to: false if you do not want to append a timestamp. This is the default. true if you want to append a timestamp in the following format: yyyyMMddHHmmss.

output_parameters.name: status
output_parameters.description: String Status of the move. A value of: true indicates that the move was successful. false indicates that the move failed.

output_parameters.name: targetFileName
output_parameters.description: String Fully qualified path of the target file.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:moveFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canReadPaths parameter and the input targetDirectory against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canWritePaths parameter. If the specified file name or directory is not in the respective allowed lists, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameters, see Server Configuration Parameters If the service cannot move the file by using the default move operation in Java, the service copies the file from the source directory to the destination directory, and then removes the file from the source directory. If a file with the same name as the fileName parameter exists in the target directory, the pub.file:moveFile service throws an error.

services.name: pub.file:readerToFile
services.description: WmPublic. Reads data from a java.io.Reader object and writes it to a file.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String Fully qualified name of the file to which the data is to be written.

input_parameters.name: reader
input_parameters.description: java.io.Reader The reader object from which the data is read.

input_parameters.name: append
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies whether to append to or overwrite the file if it already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if the file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if the file already exists. Set to: true to append if the file already exists. false to overwrite if the file already exists.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception. If no value is specified or if the encoding is set to autoDetect, the default operating system encoding is used.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:readerToFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canWritePaths parameter. If the specified path is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters The readerToFile service does not automatically close the reader object. To close the reader, use the pub.io:close service.

services.name: pub.file:streamToFile
services.description: WmPublic. Writes the data in the InputStream to a file.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String[] Fully qualified name of the file to be written to.

input_parameters.name: stream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The stream from which data is read.

input_parameters.name: append
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies whether to append or overwrite if the specified file already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if the file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if the file already exists. Set to: true to append if the file already exists. false to overwrite if the file already exists.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:streamToFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canWritePaths parameter. If the specified path is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters The streamToFile service does not automatically close the stream object. To close the input stream, use the pub.io:close service.

services.name: pub.file:stringToFile
services.description: WmPublic. Writes text to a file.

input_parameters.name: fileName
input_parameters.description: String[] Fully qualified name of the file to be written to.

input_parameters.name: data
input_parameters.description: String Text to be written.

input_parameters.name: append
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies whether to append or overwrite if the specified file already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if the file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if the file already exists. Set to: true to append if the file already exists. false to overwrite if the file already exists.

input_parameters.name: encoding
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception. If no value is specified or if the encoding is set to autoDetect, the default operating system encoding is used.

usage_notes: Usage Notes For security reasons, the pub.file:stringToFile service validates the input fileName against the list of allowed paths defined in the watt.server.file.canWritePaths parameter. If the specified path is not in the allowed list, Integration Server throws an exception. For more information about the server configuration parameter, see Server Configuration Parameters
